channel.title: Newslogger | Bitroot
channel.description: Thoughts, tutorials, and insights from the Bitroot team.

item.title: ChatGPT Isn't Just Getting Smarter. It's Getting More Personal.
item.description: OpenAI's latest ChatGPT release notes reveal more than new features. From Personal Finance to Memory and Connectors, every update points to a bigger shift: AI is becoming more valuable by understanding users, not just answering prompts.

item.title: Meta Didn't Just Invest In CRED. It Imported Its Playbook.
item.description:
Meta's $900 million investment in CRED is making headlines, but the bigger story may be Kunal Shah's appointment as WhatsApp's new chief. The move signals Meta's growing focus on payments, commerce, and financial services, while bringing one of India's most influential fintech founders into WhatsApp's future strategy.

Source:

  • https://whatsappbusiness.com/
  • https://www.whatsapp.com/payments/in
  • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Freecharge
  • https://cred.club/
  • https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/22/whatsapp-gets-new-chief-as-meta-taps-indias-cred-founder-kunal-shah-and-invests-900m-in-startup/

item.title: Figma Motion Isn't About Animation. It's About Removing Another Tool From The Workflow.
item.description:
At Config 2026, Figma introduced Motion, a new feature that brings animation and interactive design directly into Figma. While the update adds motion capabilities, the bigger story is Figma's effort to streamline the design-to-development workflow by eliminating another tool from the product creation process.

Source:

  • https://www.figma.com/blog/introducing-figma-motion/
  • https://config.figma.com/
  • https://www.figma.com/prototyping/
  • https://www.figma.com/dev-mode/
  • https://www.adobe.com/products/aftereffects.html
  • https://bitroot.org/blog/2026-06-25-figma-launches-motion-feature-at-config-2026/

item.title: Figma launches Motion feature at Config 2026
item.description:
FIGMA MOTION IS HERE FIGMA MOTION IS HERE FIGMA MOTION IS HERE Live from Config 2026

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2069827742800253230

item.title: Palette launches free matchmaking platform for design studios
item.description:
Palette introduces a free service that pairs startup founders with design studios based on a short questionnaire, aiming to cut the cold‑email grind.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2068995853184840101

item.title: OpenAI Doesn't Want To Find More Bugs. It Wants To Patch The Internet.
item.description:
AI is making vulnerability discovery faster than ever, but fixing those vulnerabilities remains a growing challenge. OpenAI's new Patch the Planet initiative aims to help secure critical open-source software, signaling a broader shift in cybersecurity from finding bugs to remediating them before attackers can exploit them.

Source:

  • https://openai.com/index/patch-the-planet/
  • https://openai.com/index/finding-and-fixing-real-world-software-vulnerabilities-with-ai/
  • https://opensource.org/osd
  • https://www.linuxfoundation.org/research/open-source-software-facts
  • https://owasp.org/www-project-top-ten/
  • https://www.cisa.gov/topics/cyber-threats-and-advisories/software-security

item.title: Google makes Interactions API generally available for Gemini
item.description:
Google’s Interactions API is now GA, offering managed agents and background execution for Gemini models. The rollout adds a production‑ready entry point for AI‑driven workflows.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2069108666121503068

item.title: ElevenLabs adds multi‑platform ad localization to ElevenCreative
item.description:
ElevenLabs' new Ads Engine lets you pull Google, Meta, and LinkedIn campaigns into ElevenCreative, translate them into 50+ languages, and push the results back. Engineers need to weigh integration effort against potential reach.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2069111326987673761

item.title: Cursor announces new model built with SpaceX data at Compile
item.description:
Three announcements from our keynote at Compile, including how we're training a new model with SpaceX.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2069149296436330776

item.title: Anthropic adds Claude Tag for Slack task delegation
item.description:
Claude Tag lets teams call Claude directly from Slack by tagging the bot, turning it into a collaborative teammate. It’s a modest addition that may streamline routine requests but carries privacy and noise considerations.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2069468693017268244

item.title: Valessa launches free SVG‑to‑3D‑glass converter in the browser
item.description:
Drop in an SVG. Get 3D glass back in a second. That's Valessa. In your browser, no installs, fully free. Go break it: https://valessa.riotters.com/ Built with @threejs #threejs #WebGL

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2067916411003924942

item.title: AI Is Learning To Run Software, Not Just Build It
item.description:
While everyone focused on Fable 5, leaked prompts, and jailbreak claims, a bigger shift was unfolding. AI is evolving from generating software to creating applications that remember users, maintain state, and continue operating over time, bringing us closer to a future where AI doesn't just build software, but helps run it.

Source:

  • https://claude.com/blog/artifacts-in-claude-code
  • https://www.anthropic.com/news/artifacts
  • https://www.reddit.com/r/ArtificialInteligence/comments/1u6f668/anthropic_disputes_the_claude_fable_5_jailbreak/
  • https://x.com/elder_plinius/status/2064776322979676227

item.title: Temple launches Entropy metric to track real‑time metabolic cost
item.description:
Temple’s new Entropy™ index reports a live 1‑to‑250 score every second, promising finer‑grained insight into metabolic load than heart rate. Early access is open, but the metric’s validation is still limited.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2067578443047596141

item.title: Slack shares Slackbot prompts to streamline product feedback
item.description:
Slack posted a short video demonstrating ready‑made Slackbot prompts that pull tagged feedback into a channel, helping teams focus on the most important requests.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2067314945151144206

item.title: Open Design open-sources Cursor Design AI studio
item.description:
Open Design has released the Cursor Design AI studio as open‑source software, letting engineers experiment with AI‑driven point, comment, edit and remix workflows.

Source:

  • https://x.com/OpenDesignHQ/status/2067192475853180997?s=20

item.title: Midjourney launches a medical‑focused division
item.description:
Announcing a new division of Midjourney called "Midjourney Medical"

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2067421950314688759

item.title: Google Vids adds AI avatars, voiceovers, and slide-to-video conversion
item.description:
Google Vids now lets users turn Slides into videos with AI avatars and voiceovers in 24 languages. The beta is free but pricing and quality limits remain to be seen.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2067631530890113477

item.title: Fastlane adds Claude for Social Media, auto‑creates viral videos
item.description:
Today we're introducing Claude for Social Media. Just enter your website and Fastlane creates thousands of viral videos promoting your product in one click. This is truly insane.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2067501867198996528

item.title: Cursor launches Origin: code storage and git hosting service
item.description:
Cursor announced Origin, a new code storage and Git hosting platform slated for a fall release, with a waitlist now open for early access.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2067012220832329782

item.title: Companies Might Control the Algorithm. But Soon You'll Choose the Algorithm.
item.description:
For most of social media's history, users had very little say in how content reached them. That model is beginning to change.

Source:

  • https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/17/social-medias-next-evolution-user-controlled-algorithms/

item.title: Sovereign AI Isn't Bureaucratic Jargon. It's the Next Layer of AI Infrastructure.
item.description:
Sovereign AI is becoming a strategic priority for nations and enterprises that want greater control over their data, models, infrastructure, and future competitiveness.

Source:

  • https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/technology-media-and-telecommunications/our-insights/sovereign-ai-building-ecosystems-for-strategic-resilience-and-impact
  • https://www.mckinsey.com/featured-insights/mckinsey-explainers/what-is-sovereign-ai

item.title: Meta Edits vs CapCut: Can Meta's New AI Editor Help Startups Create Content Faster?
item.description:
Meta is adding an AI assistant and a desktop version to its Edits platform, taking aim at CapCut. Here's what it means for startups producing short-form content.

Source:

  • https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/11/metas-edits-app-is-getting-an-ai-assistant-and-a-desktop-version/
  • https://www.indiatoday.in/technology/news/story/meta-edits-app-is-coming-to-your-desktop-it-is-also-getting-its-own-ai-assistant-2925608-2026-06-12

item.title: FAANG is dead. Say hello to MANGOS
item.description:
Meta, Anthropic, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, and SpaceX — the new power bloc reshaping tech's next decade. MANGOS is the new FAANG.

Source:

  • https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/09/its-not-faang-anymore-its-mangos/

item.title: Apple brings AI to Shortcuts in iOS 27, letting anyone automate with plain English
item.description:
Apple announced an AI-powered upgrade to its Shortcuts app at WWDC 2026, letting users describe automations in plain English instead of manually chaining actions.

Source:

  • https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/08/wwdc-2026-everything-announced-on-siri-ai-os-27-apple-intelligence-and-more/

item.title: Claude Fable 5 launches as a Mythos‑class model for public use
item.description:
Introducing Claude Fable 5: a Mythos-class model that we’ve made safe for general use. Its capabilities exceed those of any model we’ve ever made generally available.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2064394146916229443

item.title: Reve launches 4K image model 2.0 with layout‑aware generation
item.description:
Reve 2.0 introduces a 4K image model that supports precise layout editing, promising touch‑ready outputs. Engineers should weigh its compute cost against the creative flexibility it adds.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2062260665121919101

item.title: Paper adds vector editing tools in latest update
item.description:
New work for Paper's new Vector Editing! Direction: Will Taylor (@visualsbywlroo) Design and Animation: Kendall Gathas, Chaewon Kang, Connor Roy-Shaw, Will Taylor (@visualsbywlroo)

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2062231122184990871

item.title: Mumbai Tech Week Wasn't an Event. It Was a Market Signal.
item.description: We walked into Mumbai Tech Week expecting product launches and walked out thinking about markets. When startups, enterprises, governments, investors, and the world's largest AI companies share one room, the signals underneath the keynotes matter more than the announcements on stage.

item.title: Lassie AI raises $47M to automate doctors' offices
item.description:
Lassie, an AI platform for small medical practices, announced a $47M Series A led by a16z and claims to save each clinic about 30 hours of work each month.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2062184922090246332

item.title: Ideogram releases 4.0 open image model with downloadable weights
item.description:
Ideogram 4.0 drops an open‑source image generation model that you can download, fine‑tune, and run locally, while still being accessible via the existing API plans.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2062202208700313872

item.title: Chatbase founder shares a $10M bootstrapped playbook
item.description:
EO posted a 15‑question interview with Chatbase founder Yasser Elsaid, outlining how he grew to $10 M ARR without external capital. The thread highlights concrete tactics for early‑stage builders.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2060868914117214376

item.title: Blurr releases a web tool with 19 filters and 6 pixel effects
item.description:
Blurr’s new pixel‑effects service lets you drop any image into a browser and walk away with an art‑style output, all via a free web interface.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2061501427353288954

item.title: Microsoft announces the Surface Laptop Ultra for Fall 2026
item.description:
Introducing Surface Laptop Ultra. Built for world makers. Designed for what's next. The most powerful Surface laptop ever. Coming Fall 2026. Sign up to learn more: https://msft.it/6019vw79T

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2061329225886453989

item.title: 22 places founders hang out online
item.description:
A recent tweet enumerates 22 forums where startup founders gather, offering engineers concrete channels for feedback, recruiting, and market insight.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2059538588300365981

item.title: Pika launches Founder Starter Kit for Claude users
item.description:
Pika announced a free‑form Founder Starter Kit that bundles four branding assets and is accessible through the Claude‑compatible Pika MCP. Engineers can test it to give their product a quick marketing lift.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2060053796001399035

item.title: VEED launches Subtitles API with free credits for early users
item.description:
VEED announced a Subtitles API that supports over 100 languages and offers free credits to the first 100 users who retweet the launch tweet.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2059269493382434992

item.title: Resend adds MCP and Claude skills to Claude Marketplace
item.description:
We added @resend MCP and skills to the @ClaudeDevs Marketplace. 1. Type /plugins in Claude Code 2. Search for resend 3. Install All skills, Resend MCP. One command 👇

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2059275268276224331

item.title: QuiverAI adds JSON prompting to Arrow for tighter output control
item.description:
JSON-based prompting helps Arrow follow instructions more consistently and reduces unwanted variations. Better control from the first generation.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2059295909809098960

item.title: Jitter AI adds prompt‑driven motion effects
item.description:
Jitter’s new AI lets you describe a motion effect in plain text and get a ready‑to‑tweak animation in seconds. The feature is live, but pricing and accuracy remain to be seen.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2059277428971688065

item.title: Google launches AIventure, an open‑source AI dungeon crawler for devs
item.description:
Google's Gemma 4 now powers AIventure, a free dungeon‑crawling demo that lets you prompt an NPC to generate a web app on the fly. It showcases agentic workflows without any license fees, but it needs decent hardware.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2059343174112522532

item.title: GitHub publishes open-source accessibility best-practice guide
item.description:
GitHub has released a free guide on accessibility best practices for open‑source projects. Shipping engineers can use it to improve inclusivity without added cost.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2059080832363425950

item.title: Feather launches a self‑organizing inbox service
item.description:
Introducing Feather - A self organizing inbox! Try it out at http://feather.computer. We'd love to know what you think 🪶

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2058335516429635732

item.title: Base launches MCP to connect AI agents with on‑chain trading
item.description:
Base introduced MCP, a gateway that lets AI agents link to a Base account, trade assets, and call plugins from other apps. Early adopters should weigh the convenience against the nascent security model.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2059305907385704529

item.title: Ascii Magic launches AI-animated landing page
item.description:
Ascii Magic landing page is live✨ Check out http://ascii-magic.com/landing Built end-to-end with Paper + Claude. Animated completely using AI (more on this soon)

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2059300319331565930

item.title: Zsolt Kacso shares a card hover animation experiment
item.description:
Card hover animation experiment.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2056713047301902478

item.title: Open Design integrates its AI agent into Codex for live canvas editing
item.description:
Open Design now runs inside Codex, letting its AI agent manipulate the design canvas directly. Engineers should weigh the workflow boost against the lack of pricing details and early‑stage tooling.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2056309544997818507

item.title: ElevenLabs adds an Albert Einstein voice agent
item.description:
ElevenLabs released a synthetic Albert Einstein voice and an accompanying agent that reads his writings aloud, expanding its library of celebrity voices.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2056724239797944618

item.title: Browserbase launches Browse.sh, an open‑source skill catalog for web agents
item.description:
Browserbase released Browse.sh, a free catalog of hundreds of site‑specific navigation skills for agents. It aims to give bots a reliable playbook for common web tasks.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2056404332824944970

item.title: shadcn/ui ships a full design‑system CLI
item.description:
shadcn/ui now includes a complete design‑system package—tokens, component definitions, and Figma import—distributed via its CLI. Engineers can pull a ready‑made system into any Tailwind project.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2056372444751909212

item.title: Higgsfield AI demos Claude‑driven marketing workflow in 18‑minute video
item.description:
Higgsfield AI released an 18‑minute walkthrough that stitches Claude’s LLM with its MCP platform, showing a laptop‑based agency pipeline from research to ad spend approval.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2056411641592287435

item.title: Google Cloud AI powers Mivi’s dialect‑aware earbuds
item.description:
What if earbuds understood every dialect perfectly? Inside is the Google Cloud AI architecture our Agents of Change, Mivi uses to make it real.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2053808704772841633

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI adds remote‑control for local sessions
item.description:
GitHub’s Copilot CLI now supports Remote Control, letting developers start a session on one machine and pick it up on another. The feature is generally available and works with both CLI and VS Code sessions.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2056420570745450864

item.title: Chatly adds user‑controlled knowledge library to OmniAgent
item.description:
Chatly’s new Sources library lets teams upload files and references that OmniAgent uses as the primary context for every answer, cutting down on copy‑paste. The feature is free to try but may carry hidden costs and privacy considerations.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2056408432677142920

item.title: Chatbase hits $10M ARR and launches a full‑stack AI agent platform
item.description:
Chatbase announced it has surpassed $10 M in annual recurring revenue and introduced a new platform that bundles context, tools, and human‑in‑the‑loop guardrails for customer‑facing AI agents.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2056394356769366166

item.title: Tweet curates 10 design sites for quick weekend reference
item.description:
A recent tweet lists ten free or low‑cost design sites, from wireframe generators to font libraries, giving engineers a ready‑made stash of UI assets for fast prototyping.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2055922458876608947

item.title: ScreenCharm adds selectable camera layouts for recordings
item.description:
ScreenCharm now lets you dock the recording camera on any screen edge, but the zoom effect is disabled for non‑default layouts.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2056033761632891361

item.title: OpenCut clones CapCut, hits 45.8k GitHub stars
item.description:
A community‑built, MIT‑licensed video editor called OpenCut matches CapCut’s core features without watermarks or subscriptions, and its code now has over 45 k stars on GitHub.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2055957914561732946

item.title: Anthropic adds auto‑stack detection plugin for Claude Code
item.description:
Anthropic’s new official plugin reads an entire codebase and suggests which tools, frameworks, and configs to enable. It promises smoother onboarding but may add noise for larger projects.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2055830602683998718

item.title: Notion releases ntn CLI for terminal API access
item.description:
Notion's new ntn command‑line tool puts the full Notion API into your terminal and adds a workflow for building and deploying Workers.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2054594852818764178

item.title: Lovable launches SEO feature with auto pre‑rendering and one‑click fixes
item.description:
Lovable adds server‑side rendering and an on‑demand SEO review that can auto‑fix suggestions, aiming to make new and existing apps more discoverable.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2054549753224937956

item.title: Cline SDK open‑sourced as npm package
item.description:
Cline has released its SDK on npm, letting developers embed the same extension harness used in its coding agent. The package can be added with a single install command.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2054580767779700775

item.title: Claude Code weekly limits rise 50% for Pro and Enterprise users
item.description:
Claude Code weekly limits are increasing 50%, now through July 13. Live now for all Pro, Max, Team, and seat-based Enterprise users.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2054639777685934564

item.title: AiToEarn offers a free open‑source Hootsuite alternative
item.description:
AiToEarn is a full‑AI content‑marketing agent that can publish to 14 platforms with one Docker command, challenging paid SaaS tools at no cost.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2054531959528079430

item.title: scrcpy 4.0 adds flexible display mode for Android mirroring
item.description:
scrcpy 4.0 is out 🚀 Try the new flex display feature (and more): https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/releases/tag/v4.0

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2054463213022929032

item.title: Claude Code adds a /goal command for persistent task handling
item.description:
How do you keep Claude working until the job is done? Claude Code helps with this in a few ways, including one we shipped recently: /goal.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2054351031279186040

item.title: Claude Code introduces an agent view listing all sessions
item.description:
New in Claude Code: agent view. One list of all your sessions, available today as a research preview.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2053940934736228454

item.title: Cloudflare agents can now self‑provision accounts, buy domains, and deploy
item.description:
"Agents can now create Cloudflare accounts, buy domains, and deploy" Here's how it works in 4 minutes.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2052029139775787314

item.title: Claude partners with SpaceX to raise API usage limits
item.description:
Claude announced a partnership with SpaceX that expands its compute pool, allowing higher usage caps for Claude Code and the Claude API. Engineers can now run larger prompts without hitting current limits, but pricing stays token‑based.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2052060691893227611

item.title: Claude Managed Agents launches dreaming mode and beta orchestration
item.description:
Live from Code with Claude: we're launching dreaming in Claude Managed Agents as a research preview. Outcomes, multiagent orchestration, and webhooks are now in public beta.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2052067399088664981

item.title: SubQ launches a sub‑quadratic LLM with 12M‑token context
item.description:
SubQ’s new model uses a sub‑quadratic sparse‑attention design to handle 12 million‑token contexts, delivering speed and cost advantages over existing attention engines.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2051663268704636937

item.title: OpenClaude v0.9.1 makes Codex default and upgrades to GPT‑5.5
item.description:
Promised fulfilled! Codex is now the default provider and GPT 5.5 is the default model of OpenClaude. v0.9.1 released with big updates! 🔥 Thank you Sam

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2051616716007075887

item.title: Open Design Skills released as open-source design system
item.description:
Nexu has open‑sourced its Open Design Skills library, a ready‑made system for turning raw HTML into polished, magazine‑grade pages. It could speed up landing‑page iterations for small teams.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2051203959142895896

item.title: Higgsfield releases CLI with built‑in marketing skill
item.description:
Higgsfield AI adds a command‑line tool that bundles a marketing skill, aiming to keep LLM token spend low while improving output quality.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2051346056039039487

item.title: Hermes Agent gains Hyperframes video-editing skill
item.description:
HeyGen added a Hyperframes skill to Hermes Agent, letting developers turn the agent into a one‑command video editor. The integration is a joint effort with Nous Research and is installable via a single CLI line.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2051697813554405384

item.title: Google launches Pomelli Catalog for AI-generated brand campaigns
item.description:
Google's new Pomelli Catalog lets you feed product data and instantly receive AI-crafted campaign copy and photos, free of charge.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2051693177053340073

item.title: GitHub Projects releases browser‑only PDF toolkit with 90+ tools
item.description:
GitHub Projects unveiled an open‑source PDF toolkit that runs fully in the browser via WebAssembly, offering more than 90 utilities without any server component.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2051598119478108162

item.title: Claude Code adds built‑in skill for Claude Platform tasks
item.description:
Claude Code now includes a built‑in skill that can manage model migrations, use prompt caching, and interact with Claude Managed Agents directly from the CLI.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2049575802568839648

item.title: Poseidon opens early access to Numo for multilingual voice data collection
item.description:
Poseidon released an early‑access version of Numo, an app that gathers voice recordings in four South Asian languages to feed next‑generation AI models. The rollout is limited and invites scrutiny over data quality and privacy.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2049526025751855207

item.title: Fal WebGL demo serves sub‑megabyte animated GLBs in seconds
item.description:
A new Fal‑powered WebGL demo shows how to turn AI‑generated images into animated GLB assets under 1 MB, using a fully static front‑end.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2049836416289407133

item.title: HyperFrames lets you generate videos from HTML, CSS, and JS
item.description:
HyperFrames is a free, open‑source Agent Skill that turns web code into video, working with any LLM. It’s a quick way to prototype visual output without buying a dedicated service.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2048779120985645145

item.title: ElevenLabs launches 50+ pre‑configured Agent Templates
item.description:
ElevenLabs added Agent Templates, a library of more than 50 ready‑made ElevenAgents for support, sales, and operations. The templates bundle prompts, workflows, and integrations to speed up deployment.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2048794727881789930

item.title: Dot Matrix adds 55+ free React dot‑matrix loaders
item.description:
Dot Matrix is a React component library of dot matrix loaders—expressive loading primitives you install via the shadcn registry and own as local code.

Source:

  • https://dotmatrix.zzzzshawn.cloud/

item.title: Anything adds AI‑generated App Store screenshots
item.description:
BREAKING: the screenshot bottleneck is gone! Anything now generates designer grade App Store screenshots

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2047782953959170239

item.title: Anthropic pilots Claude‑run marketplace for office supplies
item.description:
Anthropic's new internal experiment lets its AI assistant Claude buy, sell, and negotiate items for employees, revealing both promise and practical concerns for startup teams.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2047728360818696302

item.title: VS Code ships a virtual pet extension
item.description:

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2047314929695310196

item.title: GitHub Copilot now runs OpenAI GPT‑5.5
item.description:
OpenAI’s GPT‑5.5 is generally available and has been baked into GitHub Copilot, promising better results on complex coding tasks. Engineers can test it via Copilot CLI or the @code UI, but should weigh cost and false‑positive risk.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2047747243617460482

item.title: Gemma 4 runs offline with LM Studio and OpenCode
item.description:
Google's Gemma 4 model can be executed entirely on a local machine using LM Studio and OpenCode, letting engineers parse PDFs and build sites without network access.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2047406475631624285

item.title: Free open-source AI video clipping tool challenges $49/month services
item.description:

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2047366820026593444

item.title: DeepSeek V4‑Pro pricing looks cheap next to GPT‑5.5
item.description:
A recent tweet highlights that DeepSeek V4‑Pro charges $1.74 per million input tokens, far below the $5‑$30 rates announced for GPT‑5.5, raising questions about cost strategy for startups.

Source:

  • https://x.com/i/status/2047605548346941643

item.title: Nvidia launches free hosted APIs for ~80 AI models
item.description:

  • https://x.com/dhruvtwt_/status/2047006444701274380

item.title: Revolutionizing Voice AI: NVIDIA Unveils Game-Changing PersonaPlex-7B Model
item.description:
NVIDIA's new PersonaPlex-7B model is a full-duplex voice model that enables real conversations without pauses or turn-taking, and it's completely open source and free. This innovation is set to take voice AI to the next level, and we're excited to explore its potential implications and use cases.

item.title: India's Tech Landscape Shifts: Why Deeptech and Hardware Are the Future
item.description:
India's reliance on IT service exports is no longer viable, and the country must shift its focus to deeptech and hardware to build long-term economic resilience. Zerodha founder Nithin Kamath emphasizes the need for self-sufficiency in technology to stay competitive.

Source:

  • https://m.economictimes.com/tech/technology/india-can-no-longer-rely-on-it-service-exports-need-to-move-focus-to-deeptech-hardware-nithin-kamath/articleshow/128120292.cms

item.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-5.3-Codex: Revolutionizing Software Development with Autonomous AI
item.description:
OpenAI has launched GPT-5.3-Codex, its most advanced agentic coding model to date. Featuring a 25% speed increase over its predecessor, the model can autonomously manage the entire software lifecycle, from research to deployment. It notably assisted in its own development, marking a significant milestone in self-improving artificial intelligence capabilities. 📲 OpenAI GPT-5.3-Codex Launched: New AI Agentic Model Can Autonomously Build, Debug and Manage Software.

Source:

  • https://share.google/tM36P3WSwQLtg0J7h
